title: Pirate Ipsum
text:
Ahoy there, ye barnacle-covered scallywag! The wind's blowin' fierce tonight, and the sea be hummin' a tune only the brave can dance to.

Raise the black flag, ye dogs! There be no mercy on these waters, only gold and glory for those who dare take it.

Arrr, I once sailed through a storm so wild it tore the beard clean off me first mate. Poor lad still whistles when he talks.

Belay that nonsense and fetch me a mug o' rum! The night be young and the grog be flowin' like the tide at dawn.

That's quite a cutlass ye got there, matey. Sharp enough to shave a whale and twice as shiny.

The sea be a fickle mistress — she'll kiss ye one minute and drown ye the next. Best keep a weather eye open, aye?

By thunder, I smell plunder on the wind! Ready the cannons and steady yer hearts, lads, it's time to earn our keep.

Ain't no finer sound than the creak of a ship and the cry of gulls at dawn. That's the song of freedom, if ye ask me.

The rougher the seas, the smoother we sail — that's the pirate way, by thunder!

Shiver me timbers! I seen mermaids sing a man mad, and sirens drag ships to the deep for less than a wink.

Aye, I've got salt in me blood and rum in me belly. There ain't a wave alive can best me.

Heave ho, ye lazy bilge rats! If I catch ye nappin' again, I'll make ye swab the deck till it shines like a doubloon.

The horizon's callin', lads. Pack up the loot and set course for the Isle of No Return — we'll find our fortune or feed the sharks tryin'.

Yo ho ho! Another day, another chase, another chance to cheat the gallows and mock the King's navy.

A parrot on me shoulder, a dagger in me boot, and a map to trouble in me pocket — that's the way of it.

Sink me, the stars be bright tonight. Must be a sign the sea gods favor our voyage.

Rum first, questions never — that's the rule I live by, and I ain't dead yet.

Keep yer spyglass polished and yer powder dry. Opportunity knocks loudest in the dead of night.

There be two kinds of sailors in this world: the quick and the drowned. Best pick the right one, lad.

Avast! I once met a pirate so mean he bit coins to test if they were real — and if they weren't, he bit the man who gave ‘em.

Ahoy, the smell o' gunpowder and salt makes a man feel alive! None o' that landlubber perfume for me.

The sea gives, the sea takes, and the sea never forgets. Mind yer manners with her.

Blow me down, the tavern wench just winked! Either she fancies me or she's got salt in her eye.

That there ship on the horizon — I can smell her cargo from here. Let's pay ‘em a visit, polite-like.

A calm sea never made a good pirate. The storm tests the soul, and only the bold come out grinnin'.

If ye can't tie a knot, tie a lot! That's how we keep the sails from flappin' and the crew from nappin'.

Yo ho, lads, raise yer mugs high! To plunder, to peril, and to never payin' taxes!

By the beard of Neptune, I once saw lightning strike the mast and light the rum — best fireworks I ever drank!

Steady as she goes, ye bilge rats. A ship's no faster than her slowest fool.

I once had a crew so lazy I had to threaten to make ‘em row to Tortuga. Worked faster than any whip.

There be no rest for the wicked, nor for pirates who owe the tavern keeper gold.

I lost me leg to a shark, me eye to a gull, and me heart to the sea. Got the better deal, I reckon.

Aye, the sea's got secrets darker than the Devil's beard. Don't go pokin' where the tide runs red.

Lad, never trust a quiet ocean. That's when she's plottin' somethin' nasty.

When life gives ye lemons, trade ‘em for limes and make grog. Works every time.

A pirate's word be his bond — unless there's treasure involved, then all bets be off.

I once found a chest full o' sand and lies. Still worth more than the King's promises.

Ye can always tell a good sailor by how he curses — loud, long, and in rhythm with the waves.

Aye, I've sailed from the Blood Isles to the Frozen Shoals, and I've yet to find a tavern that don't water down the rum.

Gold's just metal, mate. Freedom's the real treasure — though I'll take both if ye're offerin'.

If the wind don't favor ye, curse it till it does! Worked for me every time but once.

Keep yer compass true and yer crew drunk — that's how a captain stays sane.

Dead men tell no tales, but their ghosts make fine company on a quiet night.

A ship without rum be like a sword without edge — useless and sad.

The kraken sleeps beneath the waves, waitin' for fools to wake ‘im. I plan to stay on his good side.

If it ain't nailed down, it's pirate property. If it is nailed down, fetch a hammer.

Ye can't fear death if ye've danced with it as many times as I have — and won, mostly.

The stars be the eyes of old sailors watchin' from beyond. Raise a glass to ‘em, lads.

A good crew be like a barrel of rum — strong, unpredictable, and bound to spill eventually.

I once courted a mermaid — lovely lass, but every kiss tasted like low tide.

Never argue with a captain in a bad mood. Ye'll lose teeth or time — or both.

The ship's wheel don't steer itself, though I've seen rum try.

When ye see a red sunrise, best be prayin' — it means someone angered the sea gods.

There be no map to happiness, but a ship and a bottle get ye close enough.

Aye, treasure be grand, but laughter's worth twice the weight in gold.

If ye can't fight like a pirate, at least drink like one.

Storms make heroes or widows — sometimes both.

The sea don't wait for the timid, nor forgive the foolish.

Rum cures all ills — heartbreak, hangover, mutiny, ye name it.

Every scar's a story, and I be runnin' out o' room to write ‘em.

Raise anchor, ye sea dogs! There's no glory in sittin' still.

If I die at sea, make sure they bury me with me boots on and me flask full.

The gulls be laughin' — must be at us poor fools chasin' dreams o' gold again.

Aye, the moon's a lantern for lost sailors. Follow her light, but never too close.

Never steal from a witch, a widow, or a man with one eye and a grin.

A storm's just the sea remindin' us who's boss. Best bow yer head and ride it out.

I knew a man who tried to outdrink Poseidon. Haven't seen him since.

Hoist the colors, me hearties! Today's a fine day for trouble.

I can smell rain and trouble a league away — smells like salt and poor decisions.

Ain't no shame in retreatin', so long as ye live to plunder another day.

Even the bravest sailor gets seasick when the sea's in a foul mood.

That's the beauty of piracy — no kings, no rules, just wind and will.

A good ship's like a good woman — she'll carry ye far if ye treat her right.

Aye, laughter keeps the devil at bay and the crew from killin' each other.

Don't trust a calm sea, a sober captain, or a smiling tax man.

The ocean don't care for names or titles — she'll swallow prince and pauper alike.

Every port's got two things: bad ale and worse company. Just how I like it.

Yo ho, me lads! Fortune favors the bold, and so does the rum barrel.

I once lost a bet to a parrot — smartest bird I ever met.

The best way to silence a cannon's roar is with yer own.

The sea don't take kindly to cowards, but she loves a fool with guts.

If it floats and carries rum, it's seaworthy enough for me.

I've seen ghost ships and green fire, and I still ain't scared — just thirsty.

Keep yer hat dry, yer powder drier, and yer tongue sharp.

The only thing more dangerous than a storm is a sober crew.

Never look a kraken in the eye — they hate that.

The sea's got no memory, but the dead sure do.

If the rum's gone, we riot. That's just good sense.

The horizon's the only thing worth chasin', ‘cause ye'll never catch it.

Aye, it's a fine day for chaos, cannon smoke, and a touch o' madness.

Mark me words, lad: the ocean takes what she's owed — best not owe her much.
